TextENGNew: ......Hm?@k What is this...?
I had never noticed such a tiny Fragment shard before.
Fragments do crack from time to time, and sometimes they chip or break apart.
...Just as the fate we believe to be absolute sometimes develops cracks of its own.
A chipped fate is no longer the same fate.
...Then what fate was this piece originally chipped from?
I picked up the Fragment shard and tried fitting it against the various Fragments in my possession, one after another.
...And the last Fragment I tried it against was... the Himatsubushi Fragment.
This Fragment had always been oddly shaped from the start.
...And in that misshapen part...... look...
It fit perfectly.
...What on earth would change in the world... if this little shard were reattached?
What kind of miracle could it possibly bring about...?
The world warped and twisted.
...Since I had reattached something once broken, the fault line naturally caused some refraction.
...But that doesn't mean I can't peer inside...
In that world, the Showa era had already passed its sixtieth year.
changeReason: New translation preserves the Japanese Showa calendar reference '昭和60年' instead of converting to '1985', maintaining the original cultural context.

TextENGNew: ......It was a time after Rika Furude and her friends' story had ended in the usual dead end of June 1983.
``All units.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2002. The chief has a message for you, so pay attention.``
``Good work, everyone!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2003. The local precinct has given us fifteen minutes.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2003_1. I want this done in seconds!! Don't let a single one escape!``

``One of the targets is a diplomat.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2005. Don't let him realize you're police -- secure him immediately, no questions asked!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2006. But whatever you do, don't injure him!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2007. It'll become a diplomatic incident!``
``They're scum who exploit diplomatic immunity to collude with the local yakuza.``

``The money flowing behind the scenes was obtained through methods too dirty to put on any ledger, and nearly all the goods being traded are illegal.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2005. The sums involved are staggering.``
``...Bastards.``
changeReason: Different profanity levels: original 'Shitbags' is more vulgar than new 'Bastards' for Japanese '糞ども.'

TextENGNew: Ordinarily, this would have been a straightforward organized crime case.
...But the moment they discovered a foreign embassy official was involved, the situation became extraordinarily complicated.
Diplomats have special privileges and cannot be arrested.
...Yet there was no doubt that he was one of the masterminds, serving as the bridge between a foreign criminal syndicate and the Japanese yakuza.
They absolutely could not let him go.
Deporting him would be easy enough, but that would amount to nothing more than cutting off a lizard's tail.
They had requested cooperation from the embassy, but the embassy refused.
At that point, the situation reached a deadlock.
Then intelligence came in that classified Self-Defense Force radar and missile technology was about to be sold.
With state secrets on the verge of leaking overseas, Public Safety resolved to take decisive action.
...And today was the day the operation would be carried out...
``Two at the front stairs.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2007. Two at the underground entrance.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2008. Four at the back door.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2009. High probability all are armed!``
``Two more than usual.``
``That'll only cost him an extra two seconds.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2011. ...That man shows no mercy.``
``Can you hear me, Akasaka?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2009. You're the tip of the spear.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2010. Proceed with caution!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_shitsu2011. All right -- commence!``
``...Akasaka, copy.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2002. Commencing operation.``
A man appeared casually from around a corner in the entertainment district. ...It was Akasaka.
But there was none of the youthful innocence he had possessed when he first met Rika.
...When Akasaka approached the entrance to the underground club, a guard with a foreign accent stopped him.
``This place, members only.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_guardA2002. You got membership card?``
Without a word, Akasaka produced a membership card from inside his jacket.
...It was the kind that was extremely difficult to forge, and they had only been able to procure one.
That was why it had been entrusted to Akasaka, who would be leading the charge.
After confirming the membership card, the guard jerked his chin to indicate he could pass.
``...Akasaka-san has passed the surface guard.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2013. He's heading down the underground stairs...!``
``Come on... Akasaka...``
At the bottom of the underground stairs stood an excessively ornate door reminiscent of a high-class club.
Garish cocktail lights illuminated it, silently proclaiming that this was the entrance to a den of vice.
``...Welcome.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_guardB2002. May I see your membership card?``
The guard at the entrance took the membership card and swiped it through a card reader mounted on the wall.
...Bzzt.
changeReason: Japanese 'ブー' is a buzzer sound; original 'Beep' suggests an electronic tone while new 'Bzzt' better captures the harsh buzzer quality.

TextENGNew: An error tone.
...A bead of sweat appeared on Akasaka's brow.
...But the guard assumed he had swiped it incorrectly, and ran the card through two or three more times.
...Bzzt.
...Bzzt.
``......Akasaka-san is running into trouble...``
``Don't tell me they changed the card authentication...?!``
``...If Akasaka encounters trouble, we change plans.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2004. Secure Akasaka's safety immediately...``
The other guard's expression grew increasingly severe.
His right hand slipped inside his jacket... almost certainly reaching for a gun.
...Ding.
A cheerful tone, utterly at odds with the tense silence, rang out, and a green light appeared on the card reader.
In response, there came the heavy click of the door's lock disengaging.
``Sorry for the wait.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_guardB2004. Please, go right ahead.``
The guard withdrew his hand from his jacket and opened the heavy door.
Raucous music and flickering, multicolored lights spilled out from within...
A formally dressed guard emerged from inside and welcomed him with a deep, respectful bow.
...At that very moment, Akasaka's first objective was accomplished.
This was the signal for the full team to move in... and at the same time, it meant Akasaka was now alone in enemy territory, in an extremely dangerous position.
This door had been the greatest obstacle to the raid.
It was auto-locking -- once closed, it sealed itself automatically.
The door was sturdy, and opening it from the outside would be next to impossible.
...Someone had to open this door and hold it for the dozen or so seconds it would take for the assault team to rush in.
And the chief had determined that this critical task could now be entrusted to no one but Akasaka.
``...Akasaka.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2006. Entrance secured.``
``Commence operation!!!``
Across from the building Akasaka had entered, two work trucks were parked, as if the adjacent space were undergoing a tenant change.
But that was a ruse.
...The rear door of one truck flew open, and dozens of plainclothes officers poured out!!
The same thing was happening at the back door, of course.
The surface guards noticed the commotion immediately and reached for their pistols, but officers who had been posing as bystanders nearby attacked from behind, subduing them in the blink of an eye.
Then dozens of officers surged silently down the underground staircase.
...This narrow underground stairway had also been an obstacle to the operation.
And this scene was being watched on security cameras.
The raid would be discovered within seconds.
One push of the security button would make the lock impossible to release from outside.
That was precisely why the door had to be secured before the assault.
The thunderous footsteps of a crowd racing down the stairs alerted the guards on either side of Akasaka!
The formally dressed guard who had been welcoming him also realized something was wrong...!
The attention of all three guards shifted... away from Akasaka, toward the staircase behind him.
Akasaka sensed that shift in their focus through their very breathing...
........Akasaka drew one slightly deeper breath, and then stopped breathing entirely.
In that frozen world of still air, the two guards at his sides rose into the air in grotesque contortions...!!
Akasaka had been fully aware of where he stood in relation to them.
He had no need whatsoever to confirm their positions with his eyes.
...And that was precisely why!
He drove his fists into the medulla oblongata of both guards with millimeter precision.
Those fists were honed to perfection, harboring not a shred of mercy toward evil -- their destructive power rivaled that of armor-piercing rounds...!!
He retracted both arms at the same speed with which he had struck...!!
Illuminated by the strobing lights, the spectacle was... nothing short of unearthly...!
changeReason: Original 'Everything looked so strange' severely undertranslates the Japanese which describes the scene illuminated by strobing lights as unearthly; new translation captures this vividness.

TextENGNew: The man who had been watching this from directly ahead surely could not have comprehended what had just occurred.
Two guards hung in the air in twisted postures, and the man who had been standing before him an instant ago was now crouched low -- then that frozen moment of time shattered!!
The well-dressed man was slammed into the ceiling, then crashed to the floor with tremendous force!
Later, while subsisting on liquid food, he would desperately try to remember.
Was it a fist or a knee that shattered his jaw?
He wouldn't even be able to recall that much!!
Conveniently, the man's body fell in just the right way to wedge itself in the doorway, so Akasaka no longer needed to hold the door open.
``...Akasaka, moving in.``
``H-Hey, wait!!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2008. Link up with backup!!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2009. Akasaka!!!``
Even within the sea of deafening music, the disturbance at the entrance was immediately sensed throughout the club.
``Everyone, get on the floor NOW!!!``
The instant Akasaka's roar echoed through the room, screams erupted from every corner of the club.
The self-proclaimed innocent bystanders, oblivious to their own guilt, dropped to the floor to demonstrate they had nothing to do with this.
changeReason: New translation adds the nuance from the Japanese about the patrons being 'self-proclaimed innocent civilians' (自称小市民) unaware of their own guilt, which the original omits.

TextENGNew: But the guards inside the club all turned to face Akasaka at once!
Meanwhile, a small group was fleeing toward the back, shielded by several guards.
At that point, Akasaka finally permitted himself a single breath for the first time since he had stopped -- then held it again... and sprinted like a wolf!!
He leaped onto a table, scattering glasses of rocks poured over ice carved from ancient glaciers, and kicking aside bottles of premium liquor worth more than a month's salary!!
changeReason: Japanese '万年氷' means 'perennial/ancient ice'; original's 'icebergs of the Arctic' is a different image than new's 'ancient glaciers.'

TextENGNew: A foreign bodyguard who looked like a washed-up boxer recklessly planted himself in Akasaka's path.
He spat some barely intelligible slang as a taunt and beckoned with a come-at-me gesture.
But Akasaka wasn't even looking at him. The man was beneath his notice.
The only thing that man could boast about was that he managed to raise his guard against the blow Akasaka unleashed.
But it was no guard at all!!
Akasaka's devastatingly trained fists simply punched straight through the man's guard -- bones and all!!
His arm bent at a sickening angle, and the instant agony twisted his face,@|@y Akasaka's left fist drove into it!!
Akasaka is right-handed.
So the man should count himself lucky.
He got off with a mere orbital fracture!!
The man who tried to tackle him from behind must have thought this was his chance.
No matter how strong a man is, he'd go down easily if attacked from behind...!!
After all, if his focus was fixed straight ahead, surely he wouldn't expect an attack from the rear...!!!
But that would only hold true if Akasaka had actually been focused on the man in front of him.
...If the man before him had already ceased to exist in Akasaka's awareness, that assumption was dead wrong!!!
Akasaka had thoroughly gauged the distance and timing.
...Countless dangerous encounters up to this day had taught his body well.
When unforgivable criminals attacked in numbers, he had long since memorized in his very muscles the timing and range of a strike from behind...!!
It was a seamless continuation from the stance of his left straight punch that had felled the ex-boxer.
As that left retracted, his left leg traced a beautiful arc and became a dragon that devoured the man behind him!
A sickening dull crack announced that the man's nasal bone had been broken.
...But Akasaka already knew that!
His body continued its fluid rotation, and his right fist howled as it was driven into the man's temple!!!
The pitiful man was hurled into the wall, bounced off it like a rubber ball, and crumpled to the floor.
At that point, Akasaka permitted himself one more breath.
In that moment, his face emerged in the strobing lights -- the face of a demon god. Every guard in the room thought the same thing!
changeReason: Original 'the guards realized they were up against a demon' vs new 'the face of a demon god -- every guard thought the same thing' -- different emphasis; Japanese '鬼神' specifically means demon god/fierce deity.

TextENGNew: Whatever they did, they must not stand in this man's way!!
changeReason: Original 'no way to take him down' vs new 'must not stand in this man's way' -- Japanese 'こいつの進路だけは遮っちゃだめ' means 'must not block this guy's path,' matching the new.

TextENGNew: They were hired to fight human enemies.
Fighting something inhuman wasn't in their pay grade!!!
Then at last, Akasaka's backup -- his fellow officers -- flooded into the club.
Though ``at last`` is hardly fair to them.
...After all, ``at last`` or not, it had only been an instant.
The time it took Akasaka to silence five men spanned nothing more than two of his breaths!!
``Everyone, face down on the floor with your hands behind your heads!!@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2008. Resist and I won't guarantee your safety!!``
changeReason: Original instructs 'hands above your head and get down'; new instructs 'face down with hands behind your heads' -- different physical positions described in the command.

TextENGNew: Thanks to Akasaka's ferocious, single-handed onslaught, the momentum was already theirs.
Overwhelmed by his sheer force, the ringleaders all accepted their fate and dropped to the floor -- all except one, who flashed a brazen smile.
``You should not touch me!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_gaiko2002. This will become a diplomatic incident!``
But Akasaka seized him by the collar and slammed him roughly against the concrete wall.
``A-Are you police?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_gaiko2004. I have diplomatic immunity!!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_gaiko2005. I am a diplomat!!``
changeReason: Japanese 'あなた警察ですか' is 'Are you police?'; original 'Aren't you a policeman?' reverses the question's polarity.

TextENGNew: ``Good for you.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2010. How much did they pay you?``
``This will be a diplomatic incident!!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_gaiko2007. Let me contact the embas--@|@y GAAAHHHHH!!!!``
With a roar like a dragon, Akasaka's right fist slammed into the wall just beside the man's head!!

Without a heartbeat's pause, his left fist pounded the other side of the man's head, and in a span too brief for even two blinks, seven consecutive blows hammered the wall on both sides, so close they nearly grazed him -- cracking the concrete itself!!
Any single one of those blows could have shattered the man's skull!
The man, stunned nearly to the point of losing bladder control, crumpled weakly to the floor.
``...Hah... hahhhhhh......``
``...This is Akasaka.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2012. Target secured.``
A subsequent search revealed an escape route through a hidden passage that even Public Safety had been unaware of.

Military-grade machine guns were found there, and had Akasaka not subdued the club's occupants, there was a strong chance the targets would have escaped -- or worse, the situation could have escalated into a large-scale firefight with casualties on both sides.
That the operation was completed without a single shot fired, every suspect subdued in an instant, was unquestionably his achievement.
``...Secure them.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2011. Hurry!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2012. The local police are coming.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2013. Get everyone into the trucks!!``
They couldn't let it be known that the police had raided the place.
A diplomat was involved.
Officially, this would be made to look like he had been caught up in a yakuza turf war.
The small fry would be dumped somewhere convenient; only the target would be quietly handed over to regular police.
Though of course, before that, there was plenty they wanted him to spill.
That pig calling himself a diplomat would be put to good use by another division's behind-the-scenes negotiations.
There was more than enough evidence here that the pig was deeply entangled with the criminal organization.
Not wanting the disgrace of a persona non grata declaration, the embassy would likely disown him -- but beyond that, it was another division's affair.
Their job was to clean the crevices behind society's furniture -- the filth that regular police couldn't reach.
``Is Akasaka safe?!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2015. Respond, Akasaka!!``
``...This is Akasaka.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2014. Withdrawing now.``
``Always pulling reckless stunts...!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2017. The chief's going to chew you out later!``
``Akasaka-san really is a demon.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2016. ...Five men down in seconds?!``
``...That's definitely beyond karate at this point!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_keijiA2004. Akasaka-san's fists are already classified as lethal weapons...``
``Still, it was lucky none of them drew their guns.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2018. ...Knowing Akasaka-san, he wouldn't hesitate.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2019. He might've riddled them full of holes...``
``...I can hear you.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2016. Don't make me sound like a hitman.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2017. I simply show no mercy to the wicked.``
The pigs they had just captured were not merely criminals who broke the law... they were perpetrators of countless acts unforgivable by any human standard.
...The weight of Akasaka's fists was proportional to the weight of his opponent's sins.
The greater the evil, the more fearlessly and mercilessly he would crush them!
``Load the pigs into the trucks!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2019. Akasaka, get them moving!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kano2020. Reports are coming in to the local precinct!``
``...Akasaka, copy.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2019. You heard the man. Move out, double time.``
``R-Roger!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2021. Ah, sir, we have five requiring medical attention!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_niimi2022. Please arrange transport!``
Now, Akasaka bore not the slightest trace of his former youthful innocence.
He had grown into a formidable ace, utterly unrecognizable from the man who had visited Hinamizawa all those years ago...
In a dim dojo permeated with the smell of sweat and mildew, Akasaka stood in his training uniform.
The dojo occupied a room in a decrepit low-rise building in a cluttered entertainment district. It wasn't spacious, nor was it well-equipped with training apparatus.
At first glance, it hardly looked like a dojo worthy of someone of Akasaka's caliber.
There were few students -- only three or four, Akasaka included, were there training.
...But even so, this was the dojo Akasaka had chosen.
For Akasaka, karate was certainly a weapon for crushing his enemies... but the current Akasaka was seeking something beyond that.
To pursue that, a sparring-focused dojo full of hot-headed young students didn't suit him.
After drifting through several dojos, this ramshackle place was where he had finally arrived...
The occasional heavy thud followed by the rattle of chains was the sound of Akasaka's punching bag groaning under his blows.
...He wasn't striking wildly or recklessly.
He deliberated deeply, refined his intent to its utmost, and then...@|@y struck!
It was less a sound than a deep, low vibration.
Beads of sweat scattered, the dusty air billowed upward, silently attesting to the weight of the blow.
A magnificent straight punch embodying the principle of one strike, one kill.
And yet, Akasaka seemed somehow unsatisfied.
He closed his eyes thinly... adjusted the way he channeled his strength and the depth of his breathing, sometimes pausing long enough to hear the cry of cicadas, then struck again.
To an amateur's eye, it might have looked as though he were taking lazy breaks between punches.
...But the fellow students training alongside him, stealing glances at his work, felt not just awe but a reverence beyond fear for those meticulously honed blows...
``...I heard Akasaka-san works for the police...?``
``Seriously...?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_monkaB2002. If someone like him is a cop, I never want to break the law...``
``Still... Akasaka-san's concentration is incredible.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_monkaC2002. That man doesn't seem to know what fatigue is.``
On his days off, Akasaka came to the dojo first thing in the morning.
And there he trained in stoic silence, on and on without end.
This was no longer physical training -- it had surely entered the realm of mental discipline.
The other students had discerned that his unbroken concentration was the secret to Akasaka's strength.
But putting that into practice was anything but simple.
...As long as one is alive, one is bound by countless attachments and stray thoughts.
To free one's spirit from all of that transcends the dimension of martial arts and may even venture into the territory of enlightenment.
Throughout history, people have explored many paths to reach that state.
...And what they learned is that there is no single path to get there.
...Any path,@k if mastered to its extreme, can lead there.
And the path Akasaka had chosen was karate...
``I heard a rumor, you know...@k@vS20/00/VTK2_monkaA2003. Apparently his wife passed away.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_monkaA2004. The rumor is he started karate to forget about it.``

``Huh?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_monkaB2004. That's different from what I heard.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_monkaB2005. ...I was told he failed to save a girl while on duty, and it made him painfully aware of his own powerlessness, or something like that...``
``......Hey, I can hear you.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2021. Stop gossiping about people and get back to training.``
Just then, a small electronic beep was heard.
Someone's watch was announcing noon.
``Ah, Akasaka-san.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_monkaC2005. We're going to grab some lunch -- want us to pick up something for you too?``
``......Thanks.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2023. Anything's fine.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2024. I appreciate it.``
For Akasaka, having them pick up lunch mattered far less than the fact that they would leave, and the dojo would fall quiet.
Once they changed into their shoes and filed out, the dojo settled into a deep, still silence...
``...Damn.@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2026. Their gossiping broke my concentration.``
But if idle chatter was enough to break his focus, then he simply hadn't trained enough.
...He still had much more training ahead before he could reach the state of no-self.
He composed himself and drove his fist into the sandbag, but the sound was noticeably lighter than before, making it plainly obvious that the weight had left his punches.
Realizing he should take a break, Akasaka pulled a towel from his sports bag and sat down on the floor.
The song of cicadas seeped into his body.
The windows were open, but the dojo's layout seemed to prevent any cross-breeze, so very little air came in.
Even so, the current Akasaka could perceive the faintest whisper of cool air.
......The greatest reason Akasaka had taken up karate was, as they had said, Yukie's death.
Of course, being bested in the struggle with the kidnapper during that case had been ample motivation as well, but the pain of Yukie's death weighed even more heavily...
Yukie had fallen down a hospital staircase and died while he was away on assignment in Shishibone City.
That grief simply would not fade, and so he had turned to karate.
But... in June of Showa 58.
When the Great Hinamizawa Disaster struck.
...He realized he had forgotten something of immense importance.
A young girl had prophesied Yukie's death.
And in exchange for that prophecy, the girl had made a single plea -- for him to help her.
...Yukie's death was an accident.
He might have been able to accept that there was nothing he could have done.
But that girl -- Rika Furude's death was different.
She had known about her own murder five years before it happened.
When the Great Hinamizawa Disaster occurred, he remembered Ooishi, who had looked after him back then, and called to check on him... and that was how he learned everything.
The moment he learned the truth, he was consumed by profound remorse.
Could the girl named Rika Furude have been the turning point of his entire life?
If he had believed her prophecy and taken action, perhaps Yukie would never have fallen down those stairs.

He had become a police officer to uphold justice, yet he had failed to recognize a single girl's cry for help. Despite having five full years to act, he had let the conspiracy surrounding her slip by unnoticed, and she had been killed.
That visit to Hinamizawa for the kidnapping case of the minister's grandson... there had unquestionably been a fork in the road of fate.
If only he had listened more carefully to that girl's words...
Yukie would not have died.
And he could have rushed to Rika Furude's rescue.
...Those two regrets... they gnawed at him ceaselessly, impossible to shake...

The series of mysterious deaths in Hinamizawa that Ooishi had told him about had been swept under the rug by the Great Disaster, but... it was clearly a bizarre chain of events hinting at some vast conspiracy.
To challenge such a thing required the experience born of countless complex cases and a razor-sharp strength capable of confronting any great evil alone.
...He was now pouring himself into work and training to attain that strength... but it was all too late.
Everything was already over...
While he was tormented by this, the dojo's master had counseled him.
What's done is done. Nothing can change that.
...Then at the very least.
If the exact same thing were ever to happen again, he should simply forge himself so that next time, he would save them without fail.
It was all too late.
Everything was already over.
Yukie had died in an accident.@k Hinamizawa had been wiped out by the gas disaster.
...And Rika-chan had been murdered -- her intestines torn out while she was still alive...
Damn it!!!!
Consumed by Yukie's death, he hadn't been able to hear the pleas of a girl he might have saved...!
No -- if he had listened, he might have prevented Yukie's accident as well...!
...He couldn't accept it...@k He couldn't let it go...!!
Damn iiiit...!!!
Tearing at his hair in anguish, Akasaka looked up... and there, watching over the dojo, was the household shrine.
Akasaka had always held respect for the gods and the Buddha, but he had never once turned to divine prayer in times of hardship.
...And yet, for the first time, Akasaka felt the urge to pray.
If it were something within his own power, he would never pray.
...Because it was beyond his power, he prayed.
It was a terribly... terribly selfish wish.
If such a wish were permitted, surely countless people the world over would wish the very same thing.
``...One more time...@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2028. Let me go back to that time, one more time...@k@vS20/13/VTK2_akasaka2029. If I could... this time, I swear I'll save them both...!!``
...At that moment, Akasaka's entire body suddenly felt light...
No, it was more like a faint loss of equilibrium...
Like the sensation just before collapsing from anemia.
Perhaps the combination of intense training and suddenly shouting had starved his brain of oxygen.
And yet, somehow, he couldn't tear his eyes away from the shrine...
``...Thank you, Akasaka.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2022. ...Your feelings were something we needed so very much.``
......Was this a hallucination brought on by oxygen deprivation?
Akasaka felt as though someone he had never seen, never met, was speaking to him.

``We must wish for a miracle... and you must wish for a miracle too. Otherwise, a miracle can never occur.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2024. ...Because a miracle will not happen unless everyone wishes for it.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2025. Unless both sides reach out toward each other, their hands will never meet.``
......He didn't understand what she was saying, but... he felt he must not let a single word escape him.
...He felt that what she was telling him was something tremendously important.
``...Akasaka.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2027. If you, too, are willing to wish for it... then a miracle for you.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2028. And a miracle for us.``

``...I will simply return this chipped Fragment to where it belongs.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2036. ...Even that alone will surely be a great miracle.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2037. ...Though you probably won't even realize it happened.``
